title: Acridine Orange Staining Protocol
author: Dr. Steven Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ib3caqn

steps.title: Form a meniscus with Milli-Q H...
steps.instruction: Form a meniscus with Milli-Q H 2 O (used with freshwater samples) or ESAW (saltwater samples) on the vacuum round

steps.title: Place 0
steps.instruction: Place 0.45 µm HAWP filter on meniscus and turn on vacuum to ~5 mmHg

steps.title: Make another bubble with Milli-Q H...
steps.instruction: Make another bubble with Milli-Q H 2 O or ESAW on the filter

steps.title: Place a 0
steps.instruction: Place a 0.2 µm GTBP filter onto the bubble

steps.title: Place the filter tower over the filter
steps.instruction: Place the filter tower over the filter

steps.title: Pour 2 mL of a preserved...
steps.instruction: Pour 2 mL of a preserved water sample (or a sample which has been diluted to 2 mL with Milli-Q or ESAW) onto the filter

steps.title: Add 10 µL of 1% w/v...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of 1% w/v filtered Acridine Orange (AO) and mix Acridine orange Fisher Scientific Catalog #AC423340010

steps.title: Keep the AO in a dark...
steps.instruction: Keep the AO in a dark place when not using it, and use it under low light conditions

steps.title: Vacuum and rinse with ~2 mL...
steps.instruction: Vacuum and rinse with ~2 mL Milli-Q H 2 O or ESAW when the meniscus of the sample reaches the filter surface

steps.title: When the entire sample has been...
steps.instruction: When the entire sample has been filtered, remove the filter tower

steps.title: Remove the membrane filter while still...
steps.instruction: Remove the membrane filter while still under a vacuum and place onto a prepared slide To prepare a slide, place 5 small drops of immersion oil on a cover slip and one drop on the slide. Place the cover slip on the slide. Before laying the filter on the slide, lift off the cover slip.

steps.title: Replace the cover slip and remove any air bubbles
steps.instruction: Replace the cover slip and remove any air bubbles

steps.title: View the slides with the Leica...
steps.instruction: View the slides with the Leica DM4000-6000 epifluorescent microscope using the L5 (blue) fillter at excitation = 482 nm and emission = 525 nm
